title: The 11 Best Herbal Tinctures for Psoriasis and Skin Health

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also has antioxidant effects that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

Tannins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, thereby providing relief from the symptoms of this chronic skin condition.
Kaempferol is good for psoriasis because it helps to reduce inflammation and prevent the overproduction of skin cells, which are common issues in people with psoriasis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, is a plant-based remedy that has been traditionally used to help manage psoriasis symptoms. The leaves of the stinging nettle plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin. The tincture made from these leaves can be applied topically to reduce redness and inflammation, and to promote healing of the skin. Urtica dioica tincture may also help to reduce the severity and frequency of psoriasis outbreaks by reducing the immune system's abnormal response to skin cells. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidant properties may help to protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture that may help with psoriasis, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 80% ethanol or vodka, and a glass jar. Begin by measuring out 2-3 tablespoons of the dried root and placing it in the jar. Next, pour in 8-10 ounces of 80% ethanol or vodka, making sure the root is completely covered. Secure the lid and let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the liquid extract from the root. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. When using the tincture, take 20-30 drops sublingually (under the tongue) 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional. Note that it's essential to consult with a healthcare expert before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for psoriasis

Anthraquinones are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, and also promote the healing of skin cells, making them a potential natural treatment for the condition.

Flavonoids are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling, promote skin healing, and protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.
Aloin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm itchy, red skin, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healthy skin cell growth.

Polysaccharides are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling of the skin, and also promote healing and skin cell regeneration, which can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms. The aloe vera plant has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling on the skin. It also contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The tincture can be applied directly to the affected area to provide relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to promote skin cell growth and regeneration, which can help to repair damaged skin and reduce the appearance of psoriasis patches. Aloe barbadensis tincture can also be used as a moisturizer to help hydrate and soothe dry, cracked skin, which is common in psoriasis sufferers.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that may help with psoriasis, you will need a clean glass jar, a coffee filter, and some basic equipment for extracting the herb's active compounds. First, harvest or purchase dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, and chop them into smaller pieces to increase their surface area. Next, fill a clean glass jar with the chopped roots, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Pour vodka or a high-proof alcohol over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the herb extract into the liquid. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another glass container, and discard the solids. The resulting tincture can be stored in a cool, dark place for up to two years and taken as directed to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tincture, also known as milk thistle, is often used to treat psoriasis. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling in the skin. Psoriasis is a condition where skin cells grow too quickly, causing thick, scaly patches. Silybum marianum tincture can help to slow down this process by reducing the production of skin cells. It also has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Silybum marianum tincture has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help to regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the skin. When applied topically or taken orally, Silybum marianum tincture may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing symptoms of psoriasis such as itching and redness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that may help with psoriasis, start by gathering dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, also known as licorice root. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid for the final tincture. Begin by measuring out 1 part of dried licorice root to 2 parts of vodka in the glass jar. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots infuse into the vodka. After this time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the dark glass bottle, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid out as possible. Discard the solids and use the tincture as directed. It is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed. Keep in mind that while licorice root may help with psoriasis, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you are already taking medication or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tincture is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce the redness and swelling on your skin. It can also help to repair the skin's natural barrier, making it stronger and more resistant to irritation. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis tincture can help to manage the symptoms of psoriasis and improve the overall appearance of the skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with psoriasis, start by preparing the dried roots of the licorice plant. You can purchase these roots from a health food store or online. Next, combine 1 part of the dried roots with 5 parts of a neutral spirit, such as vodka or brandy, in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is airtight and shake it well to mix the ingredients. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it every day. This will allow the spirit to extract the medicinal properties from the roots. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, dilute the tincture with water to a ratio of 1 part tincture to 10 parts water, and use it as needed to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this tincture, especially if you're already taking medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures can be helpful for people with psoriasis because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy patches on the skin. The tincture can help calm these patches and reduce their redness. Aloe vera has compounds that can block chemical reactions in the body that cause inflammation. This means it can help to reduce the swelling and irritation that often accompany psoriasis. By applying aloe vera tincture to the affected skin, people with psoriasis may be able to reduce the appearance of their symptoms and feel more comfortable. Aloe vera tinctures can also help to lock in moisture, which can be helpful in keeping the skin hydrated and healthy. This can be especially helpful in the winter months when dry air can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture to help with psoriasis, you will need dried licorice root, a jar, a glass bottle with a lid, vodka or another high-proof alcohol, and a coffee filter or cheesecloth. Start by measuring out one part of dried licorice root and adding it to the jar. Then, add two parts of vodka or high-proof alcohol to the jar, making sure that the licorice root is completely covered. Close the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the licorice root steep in the alcohol. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid through the coffee filter or cheesecloth into the glass bottle with a lid. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, mix a few drops of it with water and apply it directly to the affected skin or take it orally as directed by a healthcare professional. Note that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The tincture made from its roots and leaves is believed to be beneficial for people suffering from psoriasis. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches on the skin. Taraxacum officinale tincture is thought to help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and the appearance of these patches. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with psoriasis, first, you need to gather the root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant, also known as licorice root, which is commonly found in herbal shops or online stores. Next, clean the root thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, chop the root into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar. Add a neutral-tasting liquid such as vodka or glycerin to the jar, making sure the root pieces are fully submerged in the liquid. The general ratio for this mixture is 1 part root to 2 parts liquid. Now, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the root pieces. Finally, store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed by a healthcare professional to help manage psoriasis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is a liquid extract made from the oat plant. It's been used for a long time to help with skin problems like psoriasis. Psoriasis is a condition where the skin becomes red, scaly, and itchy. Avena sativa tincture helps because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce the swelling and redness of the skin, making it less uncomfortable for people with psoriasis. It also has soothing properties that can calm the skin and reduce the itchiness.  Avena sativa tincture can also help to slow down the growth of skin cells, which can help to reduce the scaling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, it has been shown to improve the health of the skin by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Some people use it topically, by applying it directly to the affected skin, while others take it internally as a supplement. When taken internally, it's thought to have a positive effect on the body's overall health, which can in turn help to manage psoriasis symptoms.  Avena sativa tincture is considered a natural remedy, which is appealing to people who prefer not to take medication. It's also often used in combination with other natural remedies, like omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin D, to help manage psoriasis symptoms. More research is needed to fully understand the effects of Avena sativa tincture on psoriasis, but many people have reported positive results from using it.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that may help with psoriasis, start by gathering dried roots of the licorice plant. Weigh out about 250 grams of the roots and place them in a clean glass jar. Next, add 750 milliliters of a solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, to the jar, making sure the roots are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots infuse their active properties into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator to slow down the spoilage process. The resulting tincture can be taken orally, usually in small doses, to help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, such as inflammation and skin irritation, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with psoriasis, start by gathering 50-100 grams of dried roots of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. Clean and dry the roots thoroughly, then chop them into small pieces. Combine the chopped roots with 500-750 milliliters of a neutral carrier oil, such as sunflower or grapeseed oil, in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their active compounds into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to smaller bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. To use, take 10-20 drops of the tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, as directed by a healthcare professional. Note that Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture may interact with certain medications and worsen conditions like high blood pressure, so consult a healthcare professional before using it to treat psoriasis.

intro_desc: Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches. It can be painful and affect a person's self-esteem. Herbal tinctures can be a natural way to manage psoriasis symptoms. Two herbs that have been shown to be helpful are Urtica dioica, also known as nettle leaf, and Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera. Urtica dioica has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling. Aloe barbadensis has soothing properties that can help calm and moisturize the skin.  Urtica dioica and Aloe barbadensis can be used in herbal tinctures to help manage psoriasis symptoms. Urtica dioica can help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, while Aloe barbadensis can help moisturize and calm the skin. Fixing the issue of psoriasis can greatly improve a person's quality of life. Psoriasis can cause pain, discomfort, and emotional distress. By managing symptoms with herbal tinctures, people can reduce their pain and discomfort, and feel more confident in their skin. This can help them participate more fully in activities they enjoy, and improve their mental and emotional well-being.
supplementary_best: To remedy psoriasis, a combination of herbal tinctures can be used to soothe and calm the skin. The best combination includes Urtica dioica, Aloe barbadensis, and Avena sativa. Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, has a soothing effect on the skin and can help to reduce itching and discomfort. Avena sativa, or oat grass, contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to calm the skin and reduce irritation.  This combination works well because Urtica dioica and Avena sativa address the inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis, while Aloe barbadensis soothes the skin and provides relief from itching and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of Urtica dioica and Avena sativa help to reduce redness and swelling, creating a calm environment for the skin to heal. This combination can be used to create a topical treatment, where the tinctures are applied directly to the affected skin area.
supplementary_avoid: If you have psoriasis, it's best to avoid certain herbal tinctures that can worsen your condition. One such herb is Comfrey (Symphytum officinale), which contains compounds that can slow down wound healing and cause scarring. This can be problematic for people with psoriasis, as they already experience inflammation and skin thickening. Another herb to avoid is Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens), which can cause skin irritation and worsen inflammation. Slippery Elm (Ulmus rubra) is also not recommended, as its mucilaginous properties can cause skin thickening and make psoriasis symptoms worse.  Additionally, Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) contains berberine, which can cause liver damage and interact with certain medications. This can be a concern for people with psoriasis who are already taking medications. Burdock (Arctium lappa) is another herb to avoid, as its roots can cause skin irritation and worsen inflammation. Last but not least, Sassafras (Sassafras albidum) contains safrole, which can be toxic to the liver and exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.
supplementary_other_remedies: When it comes to treating psoriasis, there are several natural remedies that can help, aside from herbal tinctures. One of these remedies is aloe vera gel. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with psoriasis. When applied topically, aloe vera can also help repair damaged skin cells and promote new cell growth. Another natural remedy is tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections that can worsen psoriasis symptoms. However, it's essential to use tea tree oil in moderation, as it can be irritating if used too frequently or in high concentrations. Dead sea salt baths are also beneficial for psoriasis, as the high levels of magnesium in the salt can help reduce inflammation and itching. Magnesium is an essential mineral that can also help regulate the body's immune response, which is often overactive in people with psoriasis. Taking a warm bath with dead sea salt can help relax the body and reduce stress, which can also contribute to psoriasis flare-ups. Additionally, dietary changes, such as increasing omega-3 fatty acid intake through foods like salmon and flaxseeds, can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin. Omega-3 fatty acids are also essential for maintaining a healthy immune system.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with psoriasis often experience other health issues that can be managed with medicinal herbs. One common problem is arthritis, which causes joint pain and swelling due to inflammation. Turmeric, a herb containing a compound called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation.  Another issue that people with psoriasis may face is cardiovascular disease, which increases the risk of heart attacks and strokes. Hawthorn, a heart-healthy herb, can help lower blood pressure and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.  Some people with psoriasis may also experience anxiety and depression, which can be treated with herbs like passionflower and St. John's Wort. Passionflower helps calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress, while St. John's Wort can help regulate mood and reduce symptoms of depression.  People with psoriasis may also be more likely to experience digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can cause stomach pain, bloating, and diarrhea. Licorice root and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms of IBS.  Ginger, a common herb used in cooking, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the body. It's often used to treat nausea and digestive issues, and can also help lower blood pressure and improve circulation.  Aloe vera, a plant that's often used topically to soothe skin irritations, can also be taken internally to help reduce inflammation and improve digestion. It's often used to treat IBS and other digestive issues.
